title: relay/ws.rs: WHISPER payload has no size check before offline enqueue
description: BROADCAST and SEALED_BROADCAST both check payload.is_empty() but also rely on the 1 MiB WS frame cap (MAX_WS_MESSAGE_BYTES) as the effective upper bound. WHISPER and SEALED_WHISPER check the recipient pub_id and call user_exists, but never validate params.payload size before enqueue. The offline_messages table stores payload TEXT with no per-row size limit. A 1 MiB WHISPER to an offline user enqueues 1 MiB in SQLite with no pushback beyond the 1000-message queue cap. 1000 × 1 MiB = 1 GB of offline_messages for a single target. Trigger: attacker sends 1000 × 1 MiB WHISPERs to an offline victim. Bad behavior: offline_messages table grows to 1 GB for that recipient.
